frameworks.name: disinhibition-based circuit model
frameworks.one_line: local disinhibition gates circuit state between normalization and WTA regimes

frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: recurrent gain control implementing relative value coding in the circuit

frameworks.name: winner-take-all attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: recurrent excitation with pooled inhibition driving categorical selection

frameworks.name: working memory persistent activity
frameworks.one_line: sustained firing in the model interpreted as memory maintenance

core_question: How can a single biologically plausible circuit jointly implement normalization-based valuation and winner-take-all selection during decision making?
key_assumptions: decision computations combine relative value coding and categorical selection
disinhibition acts as a local gating mechanism for circuit state
top-down inputs can flexibly modulate disinhibitory interneurons
manipulations: circuit motif architecture (normalization vs WTA vs hybrid disinhibition)
engagement of disinhibitory pathway to switch network state
top-down control inputs producing task-dependent dynamics
key_findings: hybrid disinhibition motif unifies normalized representation and WTA competition
model reproduces empirical psychometric and chronometric decision data
circuit generates persistent activity consistent with working memory
top-down disinhibitory control yields diverse task-dependent neural dynamics
interpretations: local disinhibition is a biologically plausible mechanism unifying valuation and selection
flexible state control via disinhibition explains diverse decision-related neural dynamics
